name: Cheese Casserole Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup lukewarm milk, scalded and cooled
1 cup Swiss or cheddar cheese, shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a 2-1/2 quart bowl.
2. Add milk, 2/3 cup butter, eggs, and salt to the yeast mixture. Stir until combined.
3. Gradually add flour, cheese, and pepper to the mixture. Mix on low speed, scraping the sides of the bowl constantly, for 30 seconds.
4. Increase the speed to medium and continue mixing for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally by scraping the sides of the bowl.
5. In a separate bowl, sift in the remaining flour, shredded cheese, and pepper. Stir into the batter until incorporated.
6. Carefully scrape the batter from the side of the bowl and return it to the main bowl.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 40 minutes. The dough is ready when an indentation remains after lightly touching with a floured finger.
8. Gently stir down the batter by beating approximately 25 strokes.
9. Pour the batter evenly into a greased 2-quart casserole dish and cover.
10. Let rise for another 45 minutes in the warm part of the oven, ensuring it does not touch the sides or bottom.
11. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
12. Place the casserole on the lowest rack so that its center is in the middle of the oven. Bake until the loaf is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped, about 40-45 minutes.
13. Remove from the oven and loosen the bread from the sides of the dish with a spatula.
14. Brush the top with softened butter and let cool on a wire rack.
Makes 1 loaf.
